zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Moore I於1535年7月10日出生在英國肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 聖 阿爾格時，他的父親Sir Edward Moore30歲，母親Lady Susan de Clifford35歲。他於1560年10月27日與Joane Coxsune在英國肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 聖 阿爾格結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1600年10月22日在英國英格蘭肯特坎特布瑞享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir George Moore I于1535年7月10日出生在英国肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 聖 阿爾格时，他的父亲Sir Edward Moore30岁，母亲Lady Susan de Clifford35岁。他于1560年10月27日与Joane Coxsune在英国肯特坎特布瑞坎特布瑞 聖 阿爾格结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1600年10月22日在英国英格兰肯特坎特布瑞享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Moore I was born on 10 July 1535, in Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, England, his father, Sir Edward Moore, was 30 and his mother, Lady Susan de Clifford, was 35. He married Joane Coxsune on 27 October 1560, in Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 22 October 1600, in Canterbury, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir George Moore I est né le 10 juillet 1535, à Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir Edward Moore, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Susan de Clifford, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Joane Coxsune le 27 octobre 1560, à Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 22 octobre 1600, à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Moore I è nato il il 10 luglio 1535 nel Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, England, il suo padre, Sir Edward Moore, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Susan de Clifford, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Joane Coxsune il 27 ottobre 1560, nel Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 22 ottobre 1600, nel Canterbury, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir George Moore Iが1535年7月10日にイギリスケントカンテルブリーカンテルブリー スト アルフエッジで生まれた時，父Sir Edward Mooreは30歳，母Lady Susan de Cliffordは35歳であった。1560年10月27日にイギリスケントカンテルブリーカンテルブリー スト アルフエッジでJoane Coxsuneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1600年10月22日にイギリスイギリスケントカンテルブリーで65歳で死去した。

ko.Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent,: 영국 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 알프지

ko.lifeSummary: Sir George Moore I(이)가 1535년 7월 10일 영국 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 알프지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edward Moore(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Susan de Clifford(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1560년 10월 27일 영국 켄트 캉르버이 캉르버이 스트 알프지에서 Joane Coxsune(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1600년 10월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 캉르버이에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.10 Jul 1535–22 Oct 1600: 10 de julho de 1535–22 de outubro de 1600
pt.Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent,: Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra
Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Saint Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, England: Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Moore I nasceu em 10 de julho de 1535, em Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Edward Moore, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Susan de Clifford, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Joane Coxsune em 27 de outubro de 1560, em Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de outubro de 1600, em Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos.

ru.Saint Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, England: кантирбури ст алфедж, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir George Moore I родился 10 июля 1535 г. в местности кантирбури ст алфедж, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Edward Moore было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Susan de Clifford, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Joane Coxsune 27 октября 1560 г. в местности кантирбури ст алфедж, кантирбури, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 22 октября 1600 г. в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir George Moore I nació el 10 de julio de 1535, en Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Edward Moore, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Susan de Clifford, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Joane Coxsune el 27 de octubre de 1560, en Canterbury St Alphege, Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 22 de octubre de 1600, en Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años.
